parts.text: 1 (a) Measure the unstretched length l 0 of the spring, in mm. l 0 = ....................................................mm [1] (b) Hang the 300 g mass on the spring, as shown in Fig. 1.1. clamp holding cork clamp holding spring spring mass pin in cork bench Fig. 1.1 (i) Measure the new length l of the spring. l = ................................
parts.answer: l sensible value, in mm Sensible l greater than l Correct calculation of e k calculated correctly t sensible T = t / 10 and T present with unit s k within 20% of previous value Idea of within (or beyond) limits of experimental accuracy explained, e.g. close enough, very close, too far apart

At least 3 additional values given Values between 50 g and 600 g 0625/51 October/November 2018 I to at least 2 dp and < A V values to at least 1 dp and < V V values increasing Axes correctly labelled and right way round Suitable scales All plots correct to ½ small square Good line judgement, single, thin, continuous line Triangle method seen on graph At least half of candidate’s line used and G present R correct to 2 or 3 significant figures Unit Ω / cm OR Ω 0625/51 October/November 2018 Normal through block Normal at centre of AB P and P at minimum distance apart of 5.0 cm a correct to ± mm b correct to ± mm and both with correct unit n correct, 2 or 3 significant figures with no unit n = 1.35 to 1.64 Second set of lines seen on trace in approximately correct positions and a and b recorded n within 10% of value in (b) two from: Lining up the pins exactly / seeing pins clearly Drawing accurate, thin lines Getting pins vertical Replacing block accurately on outline / outline larger than block / owtte 0625/51 October/November 2018 MP1 Workable, correct circuit diagram with power source and correct symbols for ammeter and voltmeter. Method to include: MP2 Measuring V and I MP3 Repeating with at least two other values of V or power, and / or I MP4 Measuring time to raise water temperature MP5 Any ONE from: Same starting temperature Same finishing temperature Same temperature difference Same room temperature Same volume / mass / amount of water MP6 Table with clear columns for time, V and I , with appropriate units and P (or V I ) MP7 Conclusion: Plot a graph of power against time.
